name: cpt-handler
summary: WordPress-CPT-backed implementation of a datastore handler, plus a registrar for the post type itself.
description: Creates a datastore handler that translates a typed PHPNomad datastore interface into WordPress CRUD (wp_insert_post, get_post, WP_Query, wp_update_post, wp_delete_post). Composes phpnomad/cpt so the post type is registered alongside the handler.
problem: You already have a PHPNomad datastore (and its handler interface) and you want WordPress to be the actual backing storage — posts hold the records, post meta holds the extra fields — without writing the WP_Query/post-meta plumbing by hand.
appliesWhen: You already have a datastore (phpnomad/datastore) needing a backing implementation
The data is post-shaped enough that WordPress posts + post meta is a sensible store
Consumers should still go through the typed datastore (not WP_Query directly)
You want the CPT registered as part of this scaffold, not separately
avoidWhen: You don't have a datastore yet (use phpnomad/cpt-datastore for the full bundle)
The data is genuinely relational and not post-shaped (use phpnomad/database-handler against a custom table)
You want WordPress code to interact via WP_Query directly with no datastore (use phpnomad/cpt alone)
The CPT is already registered elsewhere and you only want the handler — scaffold the handler by hand or fork this recipe
examples: Back the OrderDatastore with the acme-order CPT
Wire the existing PortfolioDatastore to a portfolio post type
Implement EventDatastoreHandler against a WordPress events CPT
tradeoffs: The scaffolded handler implements all four datastore interfaces (Datastore, DatastoreHasPrimaryKey, DatastoreHasWhere, DatastoreHasCounts) using WordPress core APIs. There is no abstract base — the generated class is the implementation. Where/findBy clauses translate to WP_Query meta_query, which is workable but slow for large catalogs. Use phpnomad/database-datastore if you need real query performance.
outputs: A {name} registrar class at lib/PostTypes/{name}.php (via child recipe phpnomad/cpt)
A {name}CptDatastoreHandler class at lib/Datastores/{name}/{name}CptDatastoreHandler.php implementing the handler interface against WordPress
A getListeners() entry registering the registrar on the Ready event
A DI binding mapping the handler implementation to the handler interface
postApply: Edit register_post_type() args in the generated registrar (labels, capabilities, supports, taxonomies, etc.)
Customize the field map in the generated handler — decide which model fields are post fields (post_title, post_content, post_status) vs post meta
Fill in the model adapter's toArray/toModel translations to match the field map
Smoke test create/find/update/delete through the datastore
Consider replacing where()/findBy() with custom WP_Query logic if performance matters

name.description: Entity name shared across the registrar, the handler, and related datastore (PascalCase, no suffix)
name.aiHint: Singular PascalCase. Same name as the datastore/model/adapter. Convention: name the registrar after the entity (Order, not OrderPostType).

postType.description: WordPress post type slug (lowercase, dash-separated, max 20 chars)
postType.aiHint: lowercase with optional dashes. Should be prefixed by the plugin slug to avoid collisions (e.g. acme-order, not order). Max 20 chars per WordPress.

pluralLabel.description: Plural display name shown in admin UI

singularLabel.description: Singular display name shown in admin UI

textDomain.description: Plugin text domain for label translations

handlerInterface.description: FQCN of the datastore handler interface this class will implement
handlerInterface.aiHint: Use the handler interface produced by phpnomad/datastore. Convention: {namespace}\Datastores\{name}\Interfaces\{name}DatastoreHandler.

modelAdapter.description: FQCN of the model adapter class
modelAdapter.aiHint: Use the adapter class created by phpnomad/model-adapter. Convention: {namespace}\Models\Adapters\{name}Adapter.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register the listener and DI binding in
initializer.aiHint: For a plugin scaffolded via phpnomad/wp-plugin, use {namespace}\AppInit. Same one you used for phpnomad/datastore.
